task_completion.description: Agent successfully logs into the OpenEMR system
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenEMR dashboard, calendar, or main navigation menu is visible, indicating a successful login.

task_completion.description: Agent navigates to the Reports section
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Reports' menu is expanded or selected, specifically showing sub-menus like 'Financial', 'Billing', or 'Clients'.

task_completion.description: Agent selects the Collections or Patient Aging report
task_completion.visual_evidence: The screen displays the search criteria or setup page for 'Collections Report', 'Account Receivable', or 'Patient Aging'.

task_completion.description: Agent generates the report to display results
task_completion.visual_evidence: A results table is displayed on the screen, distinct from the search criteria form, showing a list of data.

task_completion.description: Report displays correct aging bucket columns
task_completion.visual_evidence: Table headers explicitly show aging intervals such as '0-30', '31-60', '61-90', '91-120', and '120+'.

task_completion.description: Report displays patient names and financial balances
task_completion.visual_evidence: Rows in the table contain patient names (e.g., 'Doe, John') and corresponding currency amounts in the columns.

integrity.description: Agent uses the OpenEMR web interface
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots show a web browser interface interacting with the OpenEMR application, not a terminal or database client.

integrity.description: Agent does not manually type the report
integrity.visual_evidence: The report is not being typed into a text editor, word processor, or spreadsheet software by the agent.

integrity.description: Report appears as a native OpenEMR table
integrity.visual_evidence: The output matches the visual style of OpenEMR tables (fonts, borders, layout) rather than a plain text dump or external tool output.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The standard OpenEMR aging buckets are '0-30', '31-60', '61-90', '91-120', and '120+'. The report is typically titled 'Collections Report' or 'Account Receivable' and is found under Reports > Financial or Reports > Billing.
